heading: University Nuclear Leadership Program
label: 16274a University Nuclear Leadership Program
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Energy and Water Development and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2009, and also as part of the Omnibus Appropriations Act, 2009, and not as part of the Energy Policy Act of 2005 which comprises this chapter.

B, title VI, § 10745(a)136 Stat. 1721Aug. 9, 2022“The purpose of this section [amending this section] is to support a diverse workforce for the complex landscape associated with effective and equitable development of advanced nuclear energy technologies, including interdisciplinary research to enable positive impacts and avoid potential negative impacts across the lifespan of nuclear energy technologies.”

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary of Energy, the Administrator of the National Nuclear Security Administration, and the Chairman of the Commission shall jointly establish a program, to be known as the “University Nuclear Leadership Program”.

children.label: A
children.text: relevant fuel cycle technologies;

children.label: B
children.text: project management; and

children.label: C
children.text: advanced construction, manufacturing, and fabrication methods.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), amounts made available to carry out the Program may be used to provide financial assistance for a scholarship, fellowship, or multiyear research and development project that does not align directly with a programmatic mission of the Department of Energy, if the activity for which assistance is provided would facilitate the maintenance of the discipline of nuclear science or engineering, which may include nontechnical nuclear research..1So in original.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commission shall establish, as a subprogram of the Program, a nuclear energy traineeship subprogram under which the Commission, in coordination with institutions of higher education and trade schools, shall competitively award traineeships that provide focused training to meet critical mission needs of the Commission and nuclear workforce needs, including needs relating to the nuclear tradecraft workforce.

children.label: i
children.text: nuclear workforce needs; and

children.label: ii
children.text: critical mission needs of the Commission;

children.label: i
children.text: National Laboratories;

children.label: ii
children.text: institutions of higher education;

children.label: iii
children.text: trade schools;

children.label: iv
children.text: the nuclear energy industry; and

children.label: v
children.text: other entities, as the Commission determines to be appropriate; and

children.label: i
children.text: address the workforce needs of the nuclear energy community; and

children.label: ii
children.text: support critical mission needs of the Commission.

children.heading: Advanced nuclear reactor
children.label: 1 Advanced nuclear reactor
children.text: The term “advanced nuclear reactor” has the meaning given the term in .section 16271(b) of this title

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 2 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Nuclear Regulatory Commission.

children.heading: Institution of higher education2So in original. Another par. (3) follows par. (5).2
children.label: 3 Institution of higher education2So in original. Another par. (3) follows par. (5).2
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given the term in .section 15801 of this title

children.heading: National Laboratory
children.label: 4 National Laboratory
children.text: The term “National Laboratory” has the meaning given the term in .section 16271(b) of this title

children.heading: Program
children.label: 5 Program
children.text: The term “Program” means the University Nuclear Leadership Program established under this section.

children.heading: Nontechnical nuclear research3So in original. Another par. (3) follows par. (2).3
children.label: 3 Nontechnical nuclear research3So in original. Another par. (3) follows par. (2).3
children.text: The term “nontechnical nuclear research” means research with specializations such as social sciences or law that can support an increase in community engagement, participation, and confidence in nuclear energy systems, including the navigation of the licensing required for advanced reactor deployment, aligned with the objectives in .section 16271(a)(2) of this title
$45,000,000 to the Secretary of Energy, of which $15,000,000 shall be for use by the Administrator of the National Nuclear Security Administration; and
$15,000,000 to the Commission.
